message:
feat(init): auto-add 'landing the plane' instructions to AGENTS.md

Closes GH#516: bd init now automatically:
- Creates AGENTS.md and @AGENTS.md with landing-the-plane instructions if they don't exist
- Appends the instructions to existing files if they don't have them
- Skips if the section already exists (idempotent)
- Skips in stealth mode (user wants invisible setup)

The landing-the-plane instructions ensure AI agents properly complete
their work sessions by pushing all changes to remote before ending.

 {"id":"bd-3tfh","title":"Benchmark Helper Functions","description":"Extend existing benchmark helpers in internal/storage/sqlite/bench_helpers_test.go (or create if organizing separately).

Existing helper (in compact_bench_test.go):
- setupBenchDB(tb) - Creates temp SQLite database with basic config
  * Used by compact and cycle benchmarks
  * Returns (*SQLiteStorage, cleanup func())

New helpers to add:
- setupLargeBenchDB(b *testing.B) storage.Storage
  * Creates 10K issue database using LargeSQLite fixture
  * Returns configured storage instance
  
- setupXLargeBenchDB(b *testing.B) storage.Storage
  * Creates 20K issue database using XLargeSQLite fixture
  * Returns configured storage instance

Implementation options:
1. Add to existing compact_bench_test.go (co-located with setupBenchDB)
2. Create new bench_helpers_test.go for organization

Both approaches:
- Build tag: //go:build bench
- Uses fixture generator from internal/testutil/fixtures
- Follows existing setupBenchDB() pattern
- Handles database cleanup

These helpers reduce duplication across new benchmark functions and provide consistent large-scale database setup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:22:55.694834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-13T23:13:41.244758-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-13T23:13:41.244758-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tfh","depends_on_id":"bd-m62x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:24:02.632994-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-44d0","title":"WASM port of bd for Claude Code Web sandboxes","description":"Enable beads to work in Claude Code Web sandboxes by compiling bd to WebAssembly.

## Problem
Claude Code Web sandboxes cannot install bd CLI due to network restrictions:
- GitHub releases return 403
- go install fails with DNS errors
- Binary cannot be downloaded

## Solution
Compile bd Go codebase to WASM, publish to npm as drop-in replacement.

## Technical Approach
- Use GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm to compile bd
- modernc.org/sqlite already supports js/wasm target
- Publish to npm as bd-wasm package
- Full feature parity with bd CLI

## Success Criteria
- bd-wasm installs via npm in web sandbox
- All core bd commands work identically
- JSONL output matches native bd
- Performance within 2x of native","notes":"WASM port abandoned - Claude Code Web has full VMs not browser restrictions. Better: npm + native binary","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-02T18:32:27.660794-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:31.061977-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-02T23:36:38.679515-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-44e","title":"Ensure deletions.jsonl is tracked in git","description":"Parent: bd-imj

Ensure deletions.jsonl is tracked in git (not ignored).

Update bd init and gitignore upgrade logic to:
1. NOT add deletions.jsonl to .gitignore
2. Ensure it is committed alongside beads.jsonl

The file must be in git for cross-clone propagation to work.

Acceptance criteria:
- bd init does not ignore deletions.jsonl
- Existing .gitignore files are not broken
- File appears in git status when modified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:21.663196-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T14:55:43.225883-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T14:55:43.225883-08:00"}

Acceptance Criteria:
- Script spawns 2 agents in parallel
- Both try to claim same issue
- Only one succeeds (via reservation)
- Other agent skips to different work
- Verify in JSONL that no duplicate claims
- Test with Agent Mail enabled/disabled

File: tests/integration/test_agent_race.py

Success Metric: Zero duplicate claims with Agent Mail, collisions without it","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-07T22:43:21.360663-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T00:34:14.40119-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T00:34:14.40119-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zo7o","depends_on_id":"bd-fzbg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-07T22:43:21.361571-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zpnq","title":"Daemons don't exit when parent process dies, causing accumulation and race conditions","description":"Multiple daemon processes accumulate over time because daemons don't automatically stop when their parent process (e.g., coding agent) is killed. This causes:

1. Race conditions: 8+ daemons watching same .beads/beads.db, each with own 30s debounce timer
2. Git conflicts: Multiple daemons racing to commit/push .beads/issues.jsonl
3. Resource waste: Orphaned daemons from sessions days/hours old still running

Example: User had 8 daemons from multiple sessions (12:37AM, 7:20PM, 7:22PM, 7:47PM, 9:19PM yesterday + 9:54AM, 10:55AM today).

Solutions to consider:
1. Track parent PID and exit when parent dies
2. Use single global daemon instead of per-session
3. Document manual cleanup: pkill -f "bd daemon"
4. Add daemon lifecycle management (auto-cleanup of stale daemons)","notes":"Implementation complete:

1. Added ParentPID field to DaemonLockInfo struct (stored in daemon.lock JSON)
2. Daemon now tracks parent PID via os.Getppid() at startup
3. Both event loops (polling and event-driven) check parent process every 10 seconds
4. Daemon gracefully exits if parent process dies (detected via isProcessRunning check)
5. Handles edge cases:
   - ParentPID=0: Older daemons without tracking (ignored)
   - ParentPID=1: Adopted by init means parent died (exits)
   - Otherwise checks if parent process is still running

The fix prevents daemon accumulation by ensuring orphaned daemons automatically exit within 10 seconds of parent death.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-07T18:48:41.65456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T18:53:26.382573-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T18:53:26.382573-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-zqmb","title":"Fix goroutine leak in daemon restart","description":"Fire-and-forget goroutine in daemon restart leaks on every restart.

Location: cmd/bd/daemons.go:251

Problem:
go func() { _ = daemonCmd.Wait() }()

- Spawns goroutine without timeout or cancellation
- If daemon command never completes, goroutine leaks forever
- Each daemon restart leaks one more goroutine

Solution: Add timeout and cleanup:
go func() {
    done := make(chan struct{})
    go func() {
        _ = daemonCmd.Wait()
        close(done)
    }()
    
    select {
    case <-done:
        // Exited normally
    case <-time.After(10 * time.Second):
        // Timeout - daemon should have forked by now
        _ = daemonCmd.Process.Kill()
    }
}()

Impact: Goroutine leak on every daemon restart

Effort: 2 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:52:01.897215-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-16T15:04:00.497517-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-16T15:04:00.497517-08:00"}
